title: [UI] employee-form: Work Phone label is displayed on Add Employee form
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads and is visible

steps.action: Click the Add Employee button
steps.expectedResult: Employee form drawer/modal opens

steps.action: Inspect all visible form field labels inside the open employee form
steps.expectedResult: A label reading exactly 'Work Phone' is visible in the form
steps.testData: selector: label, form field labels within the employee form

steps.action: Confirm that no label reading 'Phone' (without the 'Work' prefix) exists as a standalone label for the phone field
steps.expectedResult: The old label 'Phone' is not present as a standalone field label; only 'Work Phone' is shown
steps.testData: Assert absence of label text 'Phone' as a standalone label

expectedOutcome: The phone field label in the Add Employee form displays 'Work Phone' and not the old label 'Phone'

title: [UI] employee-form: Work Phone label is displayed on Edit Employee form
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads and displays a list of employees

steps.action: Retrieve the first seeded employee's name programmatically (e.g. via getFirstEmployeeId()) and search/filter for that employee by name so their row is visible on page 1
steps.expectedResult: The target employee row is visible in the list
steps.testData: Use seeded employee; search input selector: [data-testid=search-input] or equivalent

steps.action: Click on the employee's row or their Edit button to open the employee form in edit mode
steps.expectedResult: Employee form drawer/modal opens pre-populated with the employee's existing data

steps.action: Inspect all visible form field labels inside the open employee form
steps.expectedResult: A label reading exactly 'Work Phone' is visible in the form
steps.testData: selector: label elements within the employee form

steps.action: Confirm that no standalone label reading 'Phone' (without the 'Work' prefix) exists for the phone field
steps.expectedResult: The old label 'Phone' is not present as a standalone field label; only 'Work Phone' is shown
steps.testData: Assert absence of standalone label text 'Phone'

expectedOutcome: The phone field label in the Edit Employee form displays 'Work Phone' and not the old label 'Phone'

title: [UI] employee-form: Cell Phone field is present on Add Employee form
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads and is visible

steps.action: Click the Add Employee button
steps.expectedResult: Employee form drawer/modal opens

steps.action: Inspect all visible form field labels inside the open employee form
steps.expectedResult: A label reading 'Cell Phone' is visible in the form
steps.testData: selector: label elements within the employee form

steps.action: Confirm the Cell Phone input field is interactive (can be clicked and typed into)
steps.expectedResult: The Cell Phone input field accepts text input without errors
steps.testData: selector: input associated with 'Cell Phone' label; test value: '555-000-1234'

expectedOutcome: A 'Cell Phone' field with an interactive input is present in the Add Employee form

title: [UI] employee-form: Both Work Phone and Cell Phone fields are present simultaneously
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads

steps.action: Click the Add Employee button
steps.expectedResult: Employee form drawer/modal opens

steps.action: Count and list all visible field labels in the employee form
steps.expectedResult: Both a 'Work Phone' label and a 'Cell Phone' label are visible in the form at the same time
steps.testData: selector: all label elements within the employee form

steps.action: Verify that 'Work Phone' and 'Cell Phone' each have their own distinct input field
steps.expectedResult: Two separate input fields exist — one associated with 'Work Phone' and one associated with 'Cell Phone'
steps.testData: selector: inputs associated with 'Work Phone' and 'Cell Phone' labels respectively

expectedOutcome: The employee form contains both a 'Work Phone' field and a 'Cell Phone' field as two distinct, labelled inputs

title: [UI] employee-form: Work Phone value is saved and displayed correctly on re-open
preconditions: Create a dedicated test employee via API before the test; delete it after.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads

steps.action: Click the Add Employee button
steps.expectedResult: Employee form opens

steps.action: Fill in all required fields with valid test data, using a unique email (e.g. test+<timestamp>@example.com)
steps.expectedResult: All required fields are filled without validation errors
steps.testData: email: test+<timestamp>@example.com; firstName: TestFirst; lastName: TestLast

steps.action: Enter a phone number into the 'Work Phone' field
steps.expectedResult: The Work Phone input accepts the value

steps.action: Submit the form by clicking the Save/Submit button
steps.expectedResult: Form closes and a success notification or confirmation is shown; the new employee appears in the list

steps.action: Search/filter for the newly created employee by their name so their row is visible
steps.expectedResult: The newly created employee row is visible in the list
steps.testData: search by the firstName and lastName used in step 3

steps.action: Click on the employee's row or Edit button to re-open the form
steps.expectedResult: Employee form opens in edit mode pre-populated with saved data

steps.action: Inspect the 'Work Phone' field value
steps.expectedResult: The 'Work Phone' field displays the value '555-111-2222' that was entered during creation

expectedOutcome: A value entered in the 'Work Phone' field is persisted and correctly displayed when the employee form is reopened

title: [UI] employee-form: Cell Phone value is saved and displayed correctly on re-open
preconditions: Create a dedicated test employee via API before the test; delete it after.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads

steps.action: Click the Add Employee button
steps.expectedResult: Employee form opens

steps.action: Fill in all required fields with valid test data, using a unique email (e.g. test+<timestamp>@example.com)

steps.expectedResult: The Cell Phone input accepts the value

steps.action: Search/filter for the newly created employee by their name so their row is visible
steps.expectedResult: The newly created employee row is visible in the list

steps.expectedResult: The 'Cell Phone' field displays the value '555-333-4444' that was entered during creation

title: [UI] employee-form: Work Phone and Cell Phone accept maximum-length boundary values
preconditions: Create a dedicated test employee via API before the test; delete it after.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads

steps.action: Click the Add Employee button
steps.expectedResult: Employee form opens

steps.action: Fill in all required fields with valid test data, using a unique email (e.g. test+<timestamp>@example.com)
steps.expectedResult: All required fields are filled without validation errors
steps.testData: email: test+<timestamp>@example.com; firstName: BoundFirst; lastName: BoundLast

steps.action: Enter a 20-character string into the 'Work Phone' field
steps.expectedResult: The Work Phone input accepts the value without showing a validation error

steps.action: Enter a 20-character string into the 'Cell Phone' field
steps.expectedResult: The Cell Phone input accepts the value without showing a validation error

steps.action: Submit the form by clicking the Save/Submit button
steps.expectedResult: Form submits successfully; success notification is shown and no field-level validation errors appear for Work Phone or Cell Phone

expectedOutcome: Both Work Phone and Cell Phone fields accept boundary-length values and the form submits without validation errors

title: [UI] employee-form: Work Phone field does not accept excessively long input without error
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads

steps.action: Click the Add Employee button
steps.expectedResult: Employee form opens

steps.action: Fill in all required fields with valid test data
steps.expectedResult: All required fields are filled
steps.testData: email: test+<timestamp>@example.com; firstName: NegFirst; lastName: NegLast

steps.action: Enter a string of 300 characters into the 'Work Phone' field
steps.expectedResult: The Work Phone input either truncates the input or displays a validation error message
steps.testData: workPhone value: a string of 300 repeated digit characters

steps.action: Click the Save/Submit button
steps.expectedResult: Either the form does not submit and a validation error is shown near the Work Phone field, OR the input was already truncated to the allowed maximum and the form submits successfully — in neither case does the app crash or show an unhandled error

expectedOutcome: The Work Phone field handles excessively long input gracefully — either via client-side truncation or a visible validation error — without crashing the application

title: [UI] employee-form: Cell Phone field is visible and labelled correctly on Edit Employee form for seeded employee
preconditions: Application is running with seeded employees.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads and displays seeded employees

steps.action: Retrieve the first seeded employee's name programmatically and search/filter for that employee by name so their row is visible on page 1
steps.expectedResult: The target seeded employee row is visible in the list
steps.testData: Use getFirstEmployeeId() to obtain the employee; search input selector: [data-testid=search-input] or equivalent

steps.action: Click on the employee's row or Edit button to open the employee form in edit mode
steps.expectedResult: Employee form opens in edit mode

steps.action: Inspect all visible form field labels
steps.expectedResult: A label reading 'Cell Phone' is visible in the form
steps.testData: selector: label elements within the employee form

steps.action: Verify the Cell Phone input field is present and interactive
steps.expectedResult: The Cell Phone input field is rendered and can receive focus/input
steps.testData: selector: input associated with 'Cell Phone' label

expectedOutcome: The 'Cell Phone' field is visible and interactive in the Edit Employee form for an existing seeded employee

title: [UI] employee-form: Work Phone and Cell Phone fields are independent — editing one does not affect the other
preconditions: Create a dedicated test employee via API before the test; delete it after.

steps.action: Navigate to /
steps.expectedResult: Employee list page loads

steps.action: Click the Add Employee button
steps.expectedResult: Employee form opens

steps.action: Fill in all required fields with valid test data using a unique email
steps.expectedResult: All required fields are filled
steps.testData: email: test+<timestamp>@example.com; firstName: IndepFirst; lastName: IndepLast

steps.action: Enter a distinct value into the 'Work Phone' field
steps.expectedResult: Work Phone field shows the entered value

steps.action: Enter a different distinct value into the 'Cell Phone' field
steps.expectedResult: Cell Phone field shows its own entered value; Work Phone field still shows '111-111-1111' unchanged

steps.action: Submit the form by clicking the Save/Submit button
steps.expectedResult: Form submits successfully with a success notification

steps.action: Search/filter for the newly created employee by name and click their row to re-open the form
steps.expectedResult: Employee form opens in edit mode
steps.testData: search by firstName and lastName used in step 3

steps.action: Verify the 'Work Phone' field value and the 'Cell Phone' field value independently
steps.expectedResult: 'Work Phone' displays '111-111-1111' and 'Cell Phone' displays '222-222-2222' — each field retains its own distinct value

expectedOutcome: Work Phone and Cell Phone are fully independent fields — values entered in each are stored and retrieved separately without cross-contamination
